system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

vertices

    (0    0    0.001)
    (0.02 0    0.001)
    (0.02 0.02 0.001)
    (0    0.02 0.001)
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (40 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    fuel
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }
    air
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-06;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-04;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phid,p)     Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(meshPhi,p)  Gauss upwind;
    div((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U)))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='chemistryProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      chemistryProperties;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

chemistryType
{
    solver          ode;
    method          standard;
}

chemistry       on;

initialChemicalTimeStep 1e-07;

odeCoeffs
{
    solver          seulex;
    absTol          1e-12;
    relTol          1e-01;
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.reactingFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/3/constant/physicalProperties"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/3/constant/physicalProperties from line 12 to line 36.

    From function const Foam::dictionary& Foam::dictionary::subDict(const Foam::word&) const
    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 1008.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error message is:

```
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary ".../constant/physicalProperties"
```

This means OpenFOAM is looking for a `CH4` sub-dictionary (containing thermodynamic data for the CH4 species) inside `physicalProperties`, but it cannot find it. In OpenFOAM 10 (Foundation version), when using `multiComponentMixture`, the framework expects individual species entries (with their thermodynamic properties) to be defined directly in the `physicalProperties` file, rather than being discovered solely through the chemistry reader mechanism.

Looking at the history, Attempt 1 fixed the `reactingMixture` → `multiComponentMixture` issue, and Attempt 2 added the `species` list but the error shifted to missing individual species entries. Now the simulation is still failing because while the `species` list exists, the actual per-species thermodynamic dictionaries (like `CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, etc.) are missing from `physicalProperties`.

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Add individual species thermodynamic entries to `constant/physicalProperties`

The `physicalProperties` file needs to contain separate sub-dictionaries for each species with their thermodynamic data. These should include at minimum `h`, `Cp`, and `Cv` entries for each species. Based on the counterFlowFlame2D tutorial pattern, add entries like:

```
CH4
{
    h           8.893e5;
    Cp          4.662e1;
    Cv          3.930e1;
}

O2
{
    h           0;
    Cp          2.943e1;
    Cv          2.938e1;
}

N2
{
    h           0;
    Cp          2.912e1;
    Cv          2.897e1;
}

H2O
{
    h           2.418e5;
    Cp          4.181e1;
    Cv          3.931e1;
}

CO2
{
    h           3.935e4;
    Cp          3.701e1;
    Cv          3.499e1;
}
```

These values are typical for the counterFlowFlame2D tutorial. Place these entries in `physicalProperties` alongside the existing `species` list and `inertSpecie N2` entry.

### 2. Clean up `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`

Ensure `thermo.compressibleGas` also contains matching species entries with the same thermodynamic field names (`h`, `Cp`, `Cv`). The file should have entries for CH4, O2, N2, H2O, and CO2 with their respective enthalpy, heat capacity, and internal energy values.

### 3. Verify `chemistryProperties` references are correct

Confirm that `chemistryProperties` points to the correct thermo file (`<constant>/thermo.compressibleGas`) and that the chemistry file (`<constant>/reactions`) correctly references the species and reaction. No changes needed here if steps 1–2 are implemented.

### Summary

The core issue is that OpenFOAM 10's `multiComponentMixture` requires individual species thermodynamic sub-dictionaries (with `h`, `Cp`, `Cv` fields) to be present directly in `physicalProperties`. Adding these per-species entries should resolve the "CH4 is undefined" error.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.1 m/s and velocity of air is -0.2 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='constant/physicalProperties', changes='Add individual species thermodynamic entries for CH4, O2, N2, H2O, and CO2 with h, Cp, and Cv fields; place them after the inertSpecie entry and before the closing parenthesis of physicalProperties'), PlannedFileChange(file='constant/thermo.compressibleGas', changes='Verify that thermo.compressibleGas contains matching species entries with h, Cp, and Cv fields for CH4, O2, N2, H2O, and CO2; if missing, add them; ensure they match the values in physicalProperties')]
